# FitOps-CLI

> FitOps is a local-first fitness analytics platform for Python 3.11+. It syncs Strava activities to a personal SQLite database (`~/.fitops/fitops.db`) and exposes data through two first-class interfaces: a browser dashboard at `localhost:8888` and a structured CLI built for AI agent consumption. Every CLI command outputs Rich-formatted tables for humans or JSON with `_meta` context blocks and `data_availability` hints for agents. No cloud, no subscriptions — your data never leaves your machine.

## Install FitOps (one command)

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/brunov21/fitops-cli/main/install.sh | bash
```

The script:
- Checks for `uvx` → uses it (isolated, no global install)
- Falls back to `pip install fitops-cli` if Python 3.11+ is present
- Errors with instructions if neither is found
- Downloads the agent skill from the repo into every detected agent directory (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Windsurf, Cline, OpenCode, GitHub Copilot)

To target a specific agent: `AGENT=cursor bash <(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/brunov21/fitops-cli/main/install.sh)`

## Authenticate

After installation, connect your Strava account:

1. Create a free Strava API app at https://www.strava.com/settings/api
   - Application Name: `Surge`
   - Category: `Performance Analysis`
   - Website: `https://brunov21.github.io/Surge/`
   - Callback Domain: `mclovinittt-kinetic-run-api.hf.space`
2. Run `fitops auth login` — enter your Client ID + Secret when prompted
3. Run `fitops sync run` — pulls your full activity history

Full guide: https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/getting-started/authentication

## Getting Started

- [README](https://github.com/brunov21/fitops-cli/blob/main/README.md): Full project overview, installation options, quick start, and command reference.
- [Installation guide](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/getting-started/installation): install.sh walkthrough, uvx, pip, and source methods.
- [Authentication guide](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/getting-started/authentication): Strava API app setup, OAuth flow, token management.
- [First sync](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/getting-started/first-sync): Incremental and full sync, streams backfill.
- [Comparison with TrainingPeaks, Intervals.icu, Strava, Garmin Connect](https://github.com/brunov21/fitops-cli/blob/main/docs/comparison.md): Feature-by-feature breakdown with community sources.

## CLI Command Reference

- [Auth commands](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/commands/auth): `fitops auth login/logout/status/refresh` — Strava OAuth with local callback server on port 8080.
- [Sync commands](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/commands/sync): `fitops sync run [--full] [--after DATE]` / `fitops sync streams` / `fitops sync status` — incremental and full historical sync with 3-day overlap.
- [Activities commands](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/commands/activities): `fitops activities list/get/streams/laps` — list, filter, and retrieve time-series GPS/HR/power streams.
- [Athlete commands](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/commands/athlete): `fitops athlete profile/stats/zones/set/equipment` — profile, cumulative stats, HR/power zones, physiology settings (max HR, FTP, LTHR).
- [Analytics commands](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/commands/analytics): `fitops analytics training-load/vo2max/zones/trends/performance/power-curve/pace-zones/snapshot` — CTL/ATL/TSB, VO2max estimates, LT1/LT2 thresholds, running economy.
- [Workouts commands](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/commands/workouts): `fitops workouts list/show/create/link/compliance/simulate` — Markdown workout files, per-segment HR/pace compliance scoring, terrain+weather simulation.
- [Race commands](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/commands/race): `fitops race import/courses/course/simulate/splits` — import GPX/TCX/Strava courses, per-km split planning adjusted for grade, temperature, humidity, and wind. Supports pacer strategy and race-day forecast weather.
- [Weather commands](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/commands/weather): `fitops weather fetch/show/forecast/set` — historical and forecast weather via Open-Meteo (no API key), WBGT heat stress index, weather-adjusted pace (WAP), wind modeling.
- [Notes commands](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/commands/notes): `fitops notes create/list/get/edit/delete/tags/sync` — Markdown training notes in `~/.fitops/notes/`, tagged and optionally linked to activities. Persistent agent memory across sessions.
- [Dashboard command](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/commands/dashboard): `fitops dashboard serve [--port N]` — starts the local FastAPI dashboard at `http://localhost:8888`.

## Key Concepts

- [Output format and JSON schema](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/concepts/llm-integration): Every JSON response has a `_meta` block (tool, version, timestamp, filters) and `data_availability` per activity. Units are explicit in field names (`_m`, `_km`, `_s`, `_bpm`).
- [Storage layout](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/concepts/storage): `~/.fitops/config.json`, `~/.fitops/fitops.db` (SQLite), `~/.fitops/workouts/`, `~/.fitops/notes/`. Override base dir with `FITOPS_DIR`.
- [Training load methodology](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/concepts/training-load): CTL = 42-day EWMA, ATL = 7-day EWMA, TSB = CTL − ATL. TSS from pace (running), power (cycling), or HR zone multipliers.
- [VO2max methodology](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/concepts/vo2max): Weighted composite of VDOT/Daniels (50%), McArdle (30%), and Costill (20–40%). Age-adjusted, heat-corrected.
- [Weather-adjusted pace](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/concepts/weather-pace): WBGT temperature/humidity model (Ely et al. 2007) + wind drag model (Pugh 1971). True Pace normalizes for both elevation (GAP) and weather (WAP).
- [Zone methodology](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/concepts/zones): LTHR (5-zone), Max HR percentage, HRR/Karvonen. Auto-selected based on available physiology data.
- [Notes as agent memory](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/concepts/notes): Notes in `~/.fitops/notes/` are plain Markdown with YAML frontmatter. An AI agent can write and recall observations across sessions via `fitops notes list --tag <tag>`.

## Output Examples

- [JSON output examples](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/output-examples/): Sample responses for activities list, activity detail, training load snapshot, race simulation splits, weather-adjusted pace, and streams data.

## Optional

- [Changelog](https://github.com/brunov21/fitops-cli/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): Version history and release notes.
- [Development setup](https://github.com/brunov21/fitops-cli/blob/main/README.md): `pip install -e ".[dev]"` and `pytest tests/ -v`.
- [Roadmap](https://brunov21.github.io/fitops-cli/roadmap): Planned phases — multi-provider sync (Garmin, Apple Health, Coros), cloud backup (Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox).
- [Architecture](https://github.com/brunov21/fitops-cli/blob/main/AGENTS.md): Module layout, async design, database schema, and the Parity Rule (every feature must be accessible in both CLI and dashboard).
